1.7 We hereby recommended that Management direct the Municipal
Treasurer or concerned official to:

a) Carry out at all times control procedures on proper handling of cash
collections and deposits.

b) Undertake regular updates of cashbooks and made available to COA
anytime for the necessary verification and examination.

c) Remit and deposit daily all collections or if collections reached the amount of
₱5,000.00, to avoid accumulation of the same in the custody of the collecting
officers and/or Municipal Treasurers, and avoid possible loss or
misappropriation thereof.

d) Undertake regular updates of cash accounts SLs for easy reconciliation of
the cash balances with those records maintained by the Municipal Treasurer.

e) Be mindful of the prescribed deadlines for submission of reports such as
BRS, BS, RCD, RAAF and other reports duly supported with required
documents.

f) Issue AF in chronological manner, and ensures that details in the Monthly
RAAF (ending balance) tallies with the actual accountable forms on hand.

2.11 We recommended that Management:

a) Instruct the inventory committee to prioritize and take charge the
overall conduct of physical count of PPE to prove the existence of the
recorded agency’s properties and require them to prepare the
corresponding RPCPPE with appropriate remarks on the condition
and whereabouts of the PPE.

b) Direct the accounting and property offices to maintain and update
complete PPELC and PC, respectively, per class of PPE to facilitate
the reconciliation of balances with the results of inventory taking and
copy furnish the office of the auditor with accurate descriptions and
details not later than January 31 of each year.

c) Invite representative from COA to act as witness in every physical
count, and the letter-request shall be served and stamped received by
COA at least three days prior to the scheduled date.

d) Report all obsolete and unserviceable PPEs in the IIRUP duly signed
by the head of the accounting unit, inspection /appraisal committee
and approved by the head of the agency or authorized representative,
and submit the report to COA together with other required documents
for the necessary inspection and validation.

e) Disclose in the notes to FS the carrying amounts of PPE retired from
active use and held for disposal so as not to misled users of the FS,
and upon disposal, draw a JEV to derecognize the unserviceable
properties from the accounting books and property records.

f) Direct property officer to facilitate issuance and/or renewal of PARs
every three years to ensure accountability over government
properties.

3.9 We recommended that Management:

a) Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the required Certified
List of Taxpayers with the amount of taxes due and collectible, and
submit the same to the Municipal Accountant on a yearly basis in
order to reflect correct RPT/SET receivables at the beginning of the
year, and maintain close coordination with the Municipal Assessor as
regards to the newly assessed real properties and require the Municipal
Assessor to submit an assessment rolls every end of year.

b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV to adjust the amount
of recorded receivable to reconcile with the GL balance and the
amount that is actually collectible for each taxpayer, if the required
certified list of taxpayers is already made available.

4.8 We recommended that Management undertake the following:

a) Require concerned officials to be more careful in its budget preparation
particularly on income estimation.

b) Develop and recommend measures that will identify and address issues
in budget planning.

c) Adopt measures in order to improve collections and strictly enforce collection
strategies as required in the afore-mentioned laws and regulations.

5.17 We hereby recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) undertake
the following:

a. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to impose internal rules in the
movement or flow of transactions, like the claiming of checks, in order
to expedite submission of reports and documents to the Municipal
Accountant for submission of the latter to the auditor.

b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to transmit immediately all un-
submitted DVs and other financial reports to allow us review and
evaluate said reports; and deficiencies noted, if there is any, could be
communicated to management for its immediate and appropriation
action.

c. Comply with the required timeframe on the rendition of accounts
including preparation and submission of financial reports and so
timely review and verification could be made.

d. Require officials concerned to submit justification letter explaining
therein the reasons for late submission and/or impose appropriate
sanction against those officers/employees concerned who remained
unmindful of the deadlines on the submission of accounts and reports.

6.9 We recommended that the management:

a) Prepare and issue policy guidelines to address the issues on the
necessity and propriety of hiring of JOs/Casuals.

b) Re-evaluate and reassess the hiring of JOs and determine if their
services are necessary and supportive of the agency’s mission and
vision, and for the improvement of their overall performance as LGU,
otherwise, consider termination of the contract of JOs whose services is
just a duplication of the function of regular employees and/or other Job
Order personnel.

c) Hiring of JOs purposely to augment existing plantilla and/or job to be
performed are essential and necessary where there are not enough
regular staff to meet the demands of the service shall be supported with
a CSC duly attested appointment.

d) Computation of the JO wages shall be on a daily basis excluding
Saturdays, Sundays and holidays and the services shall not be
continuous as termination of JOs is needed immediately upon
expiration of the six-month period.

e) Instruct all JOs/Casuals to prepare DTRs and other necessary
supporting documents in support of the payment of their salary/wages.

f) Submit JO contracts to COA on a timely manner as before the
effectivity of the contract.

g) Revisit pertinent laws and regulations on the hiring, supervision and
payment of JOs.

7.12 We hereby recommended that Management:

a) Require the BAC to observe the provisions stated in the 2016 RIRR of
RA 9184 to prevent audit suspension and/or disallowance and the
most advantageous price in favor of the Municipal Government can
be availed of.

b) Require the BAC Secretariat to submit proof of posting of NOA, NTP
and the approved Contract in the PhilGEPS, procuring entity
websites, if there is any, and in any conspicuous places in the premises
of the procuring entity.

c) Submit all the lacking documents to settle all audit suspensions
relative to the above-mentioned deficiency.

d) Submit written justification explaining therein the reasons for non-
posting of NOA, NTP and approved Contract.

8.14 We hereby recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Engineer or
official concerned to:

a) Properly and correctly prepare list of all on-going government
projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented
during the year to allow us review and validate entries made in an easy
and efficient manner.

b) Inform the office of the auditor within 10 days after the award of
projects/programs/activities or before its start that the project
signboard was already posted to comply with the afore-cited
regulations and enable the auditor to immediately inform the
management of any deficiencies that could be noted in project
implementation.

c) Prepare the PPAs monitoring reports in a manner where all vital
information is present and so validation and monitoring could be easily
done. Likewise, the submission shall be on a monthly basis instead on a
quarterly basis to give us ample time to undertake the necessary
review and validation.

9.8 We hereby recommended that LCE:

a) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit all the lacking
documents within the required timeframe; and ensure that

b) Direct the Municipal Engineer to monitor the implementation of
projects diligently and provide concerned officials complete and
accurate data to properly document implementation and enable

implementation.

c) Require concerned official to comply with the required deadlines of
submission of required documentation to facilitate our review and
monitoring of infrastructure projects.

10.5 We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer to
faithfully comply with the afore-cited regulations on the withholding and

a) Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer

b) Address the issues on the lack of manpower the soonest possible time

including their attendance to trainings/seminars to keep them abreast
of the latest accounting and budget issuances.

12.5 We hereby recommended that Management:

a) Give importance and prioritize compliance to audit
recommendations incorporated in the AAR and so agency’s overall
performance will improve.

b) Take note of the required timeframe for submission of the duly

c) Provide copy of the said AAPSI to DBM, Speaker of the House of

d) Posting of the said report in the agency’s website is likewise enjoined.

reports and transactions documents since the prescribed period within which
to submit has already lapsed, and the necessity of its immediate audit has
been laid down to establish proper utilization thereof.

Recommendations:
We hereby recommended the following:
- The Accounting, Supply and Property Units shall update regularly, PPE
Subsidiary Ledgers, Stock Cards and Property Cards, respectively, and
reconcile balances of these records with the Report on Physical Count of
Inventories and PPE to be able to reflect a complete and accurate balances in
the financial statements.
- The management shall likewise include in their yearly plan the conduct of the
required physical count of Inventories and PPE to be undertaken by the
agency’s Inventory Committee, and so required reports can be prepared and
submitted to COA on or before January 31 of the ensuing year.
- Invite representative from the Commission on Audit (COA) to act as witness
in every inventory-taking to comply with the regulation. Invitation letter
shall be served and stamped received by COA at least three days prior to the
scheduled date to give the auditor an ample time to prepare and settle
whatever time inconveniences that may have.

Recommendations:
We hereby recommended the following:
- The management shall faithfully follow correct disbursement system and so
only valid and proper claims will be processed and paid.
- Exert all possible efforts to settle all audit suspensions within the required
timeframe to avoid disallowance in audit, and the required ocular inspection
could be promptly undertaken as well.
- Be guided of the pertinent sections in the afore-mentioned regulations and in
other GPPB issued resolutions simplifying the requirements in project
implementation during emergency cases.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer shall faithfully adhere to the
provisions stated in the afore-mentioned regulations in relation to the
deduction, withholding and remittance of required taxes specifically on goods
and services, and remit the same within the required timeframe which is every
10th of the ensuing month.

Recommendations:
The management shall undertake the following:
- Exert all possible efforts to collate all the lacking documents and/or prepare
a written justification for the non-settlement of the audit suspensions, and
submit the same to COA for the necessary evaluation and review, otherwise,
the transactions covered by it will be disallowed in audit.
- Refrain from undertaking a project not supported with complete bid and
technical documentation. However, awarding of contract shall only be made
to a legally, financially and technically capable supplier/contractor and
willing to cooperate in the submission of required documents.
- Assign additional qualified personnel to take charge in the preparation of
documents and settlement of all audit suspensions to be able to meet the
required deadlines.

Recommendations:
We recommended the following:
- Prioritize submission of the monthly report of transactions to COA within
the deadline set to avoid negative repercussion; and timely audit of its
transactions could be undertaken as well.
- Maximize work assignment among accounting personnel and/or assign
additional staff, if the present workforce is not enough, so as to prevent work
overload and meet deadlines on reports submission.

Recommendations:
We hereby recommended to management the following:
- The Municipal Accountant shall ensure that the required supporting
documents of all claims are complete before affixing her/his signature in
Box A of the Disbursement Voucher.
- The management shall likewise observe that proper flow of transactions is
strictly followed so that only valid and proper claims are processed and
paid.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer shall stamp “PAID” all DVs
and its supporting documents after payment was made, to prevent the re-use of
the same on similar transaction.

Recommendations:
We hereby recommended the following:
- The management shall prioritize finalization of the Municipality’s Solid
Waste Management Plan and so submission of the same to the National Solid
Waste Commission for approval could be undertaken.
- Ensure that implementation of the related solid waste management
programs/projects are authorized programs and projects included in the
approved plan, as any deviation from the said plan may pose doubts in the
said implementation and could be a ground for disallowance in audit.
- Complete other required documentation/data on Solid Waste Management
and immediately submit copy of the same to COA including the creation of
the Solid Waste Management Board and proof in the establishment of the
required solid waste facilities.
- Fully comply with the Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 and other
regulations pertaining thereto to be able to have a systematic and
comprehensive ecological waste management program.
